severity_policy: All issues must be classified by severity and scope. P0 issues must block v4.0. P1 issues must be fixed or explicitly reviewed before v4.0. P2 issues may be documented as future improvements. Low-risk issues may be fixed immediately, but high-risk issues must not be ignored, hidden, or bypassed.
p0_attack_cases: hidden upload or unexpected network/cloud behavior
real LLM/API/network required by core tests
KB-bound Agent can access unauthorized KB
all-history memory injection is possible by default
expected user errors show raw stack traces
Core/UI contract drift causes false UI claims
report files are empty or placeholder-only
docs or UI falsely claim unsupported features
v4 gate says ready while P0 exists

findings.reason: Contradictory verification sources honestly produce status=warning score=0.6715 review_required=True; not forced to pass.
findings.user_impact: The product claim cannot be safely presented as complete until this is resolved or explicitly accepted.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep the item visible in the final gate until it is fixed or explicitly accepted as non-blocking.

findings.reason: Update/archive/delete/rollback lifecycle appears partial. Cleanup plans exist, but destructive actions are not enabled by default, which is safe but means lifecycle CRUD is not fully proven.
findings.user_impact: The product claim cannot be safely presented as complete until this is resolved or explicitly accepted.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep destructive lifecycle actions unsupported by default and document the non-destructive cleanup/archive boundary.
